decks.title: Friend Court
decks.description: Silly friend disputes for dramatic living-room justice.

cases.prompt: Ate the last slice of pizza and left the box as a decoy.
Sent twelve voice notes instead of one text.
Claimed aux cord power, then played one song on repeat.
Borrowed a hoodie and started calling it vintage.
Spoiled a show finale with suspiciously specific hints.
Arrived late but blamed the group chat vibes.
Took the best selfie angle and cropped everyone else badly.
Invented house rules halfway through a board game.
Finished the shared fries while maintaining eye contact.
Said 'five minutes away' while still choosing shoes.

decks.title: Family Tribunal
decks.description: Low-stakes household squabbles with high-stakes speeches.

cases.prompt: Changed the thermostat and denied touching it.
Used the last clean towel without starting laundry.
Ate leftovers clearly labeled with another person's name.
Paused a movie every two minutes to explain the plot.
Loaded the dishwasher like a modern art installation.
Told an embarrassing baby story to new guests.
Moved the remote to a secret second location.
Declared a snack 'for everyone' after eating most of it.
Started a group photo after everyone had changed clothes.
Reheated fish and called it an act of courage.

decks.title: Office Chaos Court
decks.description: Workplace comedy cases for off-the-clock party groups.

cases.prompt: Replied all with 'thanks' and created a notification avalanche.
Booked a meeting that could have been a three-word message.
Took the last donut and left the empty box in the break room.
Said 'quick sync' and scheduled forty-five minutes.
Renamed a shared file to 'final_final_REAL_final'.
Mic stayed unmuted during a very crunchy snack break.
Borrowed a charger and built a permanent desk shrine around it.
Used corporate jargon to describe ordering lunch.
Set a calendar invite for 4:59 PM on Friday.
Left one sip of coffee in the pot to avoid making more.
